div1.entryFree:
Unmask (unmásk; únmask in Lucr. 940) 1) tr. to strip of a mask; to lay open: to u. falsehood and bring truth to light, Lucr. 940. u. this moody heaviness and tell thy grief, Lucr. 940 if she u. her beauty to the moon, Hml. I, 3, 37.
2) intr. to put off a mask, to unveil one's self: now I will u. Meas. V, 206.
